The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes a wide range of plants, from coffee and gardenias to madder and some species of gardenias. Laseguea bracteata is one of the lesser-known species within this family.

intro: Laseguea bracteata, also known as Laseguea, is a plant species native to tropical regions, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Laseguea bracteata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Laseguea is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in various herbal formulations, including topical creams and ointments, due to its potential to promote skin health and wound recovery.
